author: The Seventh Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Nice
book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The Seventh Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Nice.
The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.

metadata.author: The Seventh Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Nice

title: Canon XXII.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: That when it happens that monks have to eat with women they ought to observe giving of thanks, and abstemiousness, and discretion.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: To surrender all things to God, and not to serve our own wills, is great gain.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: For says the divine Apostle, “whether ye eat or drink, do all to the glory of God.”

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: And Christ our God has bidden us in his Gospels, to cut off the beginning of sins; for not only is adultery rebuked by him, but even the movement of the mind towards the act of adultery when he says, “Whosoever looketh on a woman to lust after her, hath committed adultery with her already in his heart.”

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: We who have been thus taught ought therefore to purify our minds.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: Now although all things are lawful, all things are not expedient, as we have been taught by the mouth of the Apostle.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: It is needful that all men should eat in order that they may live.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: And for those to whom life consists of marrying, and bringing forth children, and of the condition of the lay state, there is nothing unbecoming in men and women eating together, only let them give thanks to the giver of the food; but if there be the entertainments of the theatre, that is, Satanic songs accompanied with the meretricious inflections of harps, there come upon them, through these things, the curse of the prophet, who thus speaks: “Woe to them who drink wine with harp and psaltery, but they regard not the works of the Lord, and consider not the works of his hands.”

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: Whenever persons of this sort are found among Christians, let them amend their ways; but if they will not do so, let there overtake them the penalties which have been enacted in the canons by our predecessors.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: With regard to those whose life is free from care and apart from men, that is, those who have resolved before the Lord God to carry the solitary yoke, they should sit down alone and in silence.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: Moreover it is also altogether unlawful for those who have chosen the priestly life to eat in private with women, unless it be with God-fearing and discreet men and women, so that even their feast may be turned to spiritual edification.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: The same rule is to be observed with relatives.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: Again, if it happen that a monk or priest while on a journey does not have with him what is absolutely necessary for him, and, because of his pressing needs, thinks well to turn aside into an inn or into someone’s house, this he is permitted to do, seeing that need compels.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: There is no objection to laywomen eating with men: it is not right however for men who have chosen the lonely life, to eat privately with women; unless perchance together with them that fear God and with religious men and women.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: But when travelling, a monk or anyone in sacred orders, not carrying necessary provisions with him, may enter a public house.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: Balsamon refers in connexion with this canon to Apostolic Canons xlij. and xliij.; lx. of the Synod of Carthage, and lxij. of the Synod in Trullo.
